title: _io.FileIO / select.epoll: closing a shared descriptor object races with reads of its plain int fd field (self->fd / self->epfd)
description: Both _io.FileIO and select.epoll keep their OS descriptor in a plain int field. close() writes the -1 sentinel with no atomic and no shared lock, while fileno() (and, on a fresh open(), _Py_fstat_noraise's fstat) read it. Concurrently closing a shared descriptor object and reading its fd is a data race on the field (memory race on self->epfd / self->fd) and, once the descriptor number is recycled, an fd-reuse hazard on the descriptor itself. A fd-lifecycle CLUSTER of three seeded faces sharing one root cause.
reproduced_signatures: Modules/_io/fileio.c:internal_close:128 | Modules/_io/fileio.c:_io_FileIO_fileno_impl:612 (fileio face, same self->fd field that underlies seeded (a)/(b))
sites: Modules/_io/fileio.c internal_close:128 (WRITE self->fd = -1 -- plain, no lock/atomic; this repro's fileio write face)
Modules/_io/fileio.c internal_close:132 (close(fd) inside Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS -- the fd-resource write of seeded face (a))
Modules/_io/fileio.c _io_FileIO_fileno_impl:612 (READ self->fd: `if (self->fd < 0)` + PyLong_FromLong(self->fd) -- this repro's fileio read face)

Modules/_io/fileio.c _io_FileIO___init___impl:477 (fstat via _Py_fstat_noraise(self->fd, ...) inside Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS -- read side of seeded fd-resource face (a)/(b), on a freshly-opened / recycled fd)
Modules/selectmodule.c pyepoll_internal_close:1335 (WRITE self->epfd = -1 -- plain, no atomic; epoll write face)
Modules/selectmodule.c select_epoll_fileno_impl:1477 (READ self->epfd: `if (self->epfd < 0)` -- epoll read face)
Modules/selectmodule.c:1435 (@critical_section on select.epoll.close ONLY; fileno/register/modify/unregister/poll/__enter__/__exit__ are NOT @critical_section -- asymmetric protection = smoking gun)
race_kind: fd-lifecycle: close() writing the -1 sentinel into a plain int fd field vs unlocked readers (fileno / fstat). Epoll face = memory race on self->epfd; fileio face = memory race on self->fd + fd-resource-reuse race (close(N) vs fstat(N) on a recycled descriptor)
shared_state: the descriptor field of a shared descriptor object: fileio.fd (int) / pyEpoll_Object.epfd (SOCKET/int), and the underlying OS file descriptor it names
severity: low-medium (confirmed TSan data race on the fd field; value-benign in isolation -- close stores -1, single aligned word -- but the unsynchronized lifecycle enables fd-reuse / descriptor confusion: a reader latched on fd N, or a fresh open() handed the recycled N, can fstat/read/write/epoll_ctl a descriptor another thread already closed that now names a different file, a use-after-close analog for OS fds; no crash observed in the TSan build)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66 every run; epoll 4/4 runs SUMMARY pyepoll_internal_close:1335/select_epoll_fileno_impl:1477; fileio 2/2 runs SUMMARY alternating self->fd faces _io_FileIO_fileno_impl:612 / internal_close:126)
suggested_fix: epoll (strongest): make self->epfd atomic (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD/STORE_INT relaxed) OR add @critical_section to fileno and the other epfd readers to match the already-@critical_section close. fileio: make self->fd atomic so close/fileno/__init__ agree without UB; the residual fd-resource-reuse race is inherent to closing a descriptor another thread still uses -> document ('do not use a file object from another thread while closing it', matching the fileio.c:129 comment).
notes: Cluster of 3 seeded faces, one root cause: a descriptor-owning object storing its fd in a plain int field that close() overwrites with -1 while other methods read it, unsynchronized. Epoll face (c) reproduced EXACTLY (pyepoll_internal_close vs select_epoll_fileno_impl). Fileio face reproduced on the underlying self->fd memory race (internal_close write vs _io_FileIO_fileno_impl read) -- same field that the seeded fd-resource race (a) rides on. The seeded (a) is TSan's libc fd interceptor (Location is file descriptor 2): close(N) in internal_close racing fstat(N) in a fresh open()'s __init__ after fd-number reuse; NOT force-reproducible in a bounded harness (3.2M free-running open/close iters did not trip it; a barrier-synchronized fd-reuse harness timed out under TSan), but the fleet caught it and the field race is the same root cause. FileIO has NO @critical_section on any method; select.epoll has @critical_section on close ONLY (asymmetric -> ineffective). Audit sibling descriptor objects with plain-int fd fields: _io.FileIO, select.epoll, select.kqueue, select.devpoll, _socket.

related_issues.note: Race conditions in FileIO (PR #151708, fileio.c only) -- covers the FileIO faces
kqueue.kqfd close-vs-closed-getter race -- the SIBLING of our epoll(epfd) close-vs-fileno face; #151708 does NOT touch selectmodule, so the epoll face is in-scope and not explicitly covered

assessment: FileIO faces (internal_close/__init__ vs _Py_fstat, self->fd) = ALREADY REPORTED #151707/#151708. EPOLL face (select.epoll close @critical_section but fileno/register/poll not; epfd plain int) is a DISTINCT in-scope finding, sibling of the kqueue #151364 -- not covered by #151708. Audit select.epoll/devpoll alongside #151364.
